meta.section: Identity & Access — Authentication Methods (part 2 of 2: directory / device / consent / security defaults)
meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/entra) — portal paths, setting labels, and defaults grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03.
generalNotes: These are the non-auth-method items filed under the Authentication Methods section — they live in User settings, Devices, External Identities, Enterprise applications, Security Defaults, and (one) the M365 admin center.

Sequencing matters: disabling Security Defaults (DefSecSettings) assumes the Conditional Access MFA policies are already deployed and On — otherwise MFA coverage drops to zero. Treat it as the last switch after the CA policies validate.

Roles vary: default-user-permissions and device settings need Global Administrator / Cloud Device Administrator; consent settings need Privileged Role Administrator; Security Defaults needs Conditional Access Administrator.

requiredSettings.label: Users can register applications
requiredSettings.value: No

requiredSettings.label: Users can create security groups
requiredSettings.value: No

requiredSettings.label: Restrict non-admin users from creating tenants
requiredSettings.value: Yes (i.e. block tenant creation)

requiredSettings.label: Read other users
requiredSettings.value: Left enabled — users can still read basic directory info

steps: Entra ID > Users > User settings.
Under Default user role permissions: set 'Users can register applications' = No; 'Users can create security groups' = No; 'Restrict non-admin users from creating tenants' = Yes.
Save.
Note (MEDIUM impact): app developers / group owners who relied on self-service will now need an admin (or the App Developer / Groups roles) — grant those back to specific people as needed.

requiredSettings.label: Guest user access
requiredSettings.value: Guest user access is restricted to properties and memberships of their own directory objects (most restrictive)

requiredSettings.label: Guest invite settings
requiredSettings.value: Only users assigned to specific admin roles (and the Guest Inviter role) can invite — not all members/guests

steps: Entra ID > External Identities > External collaboration settings.
Set Guest user access = 'restricted to their own directory objects (most restrictive)'.
Set Guest invite settings so only admins / the Guest Inviter role can invite guests.
Save (guest changes can take up to ~15 min). This mirrors DirectoryGuestAccess and pairs with the CA guest policies.

requiredSettings.label: Users can request admin consent to apps they are unable to consent to
requiredSettings.value: Yes (enables the admin consent workflow)

requiredSettings.label: Reviewers
requiredSettings.value: Select the users/groups/roles who receive and act on requests

requiredSettings.label: Notifications / expiry
requiredSettings.value: Enable reviewer email notifications; set a request expiry (e.g. 30 days)

steps: Entra ID > Enterprise apps > Consent and permissions > Admin consent settings.
Set 'Users can request admin consent to apps they are unable to consent to' = Yes.
Choose the reviewers, enable email notifications, and set the request expiry.
Save (can take up to an hour to enable). Pairs with UserConsentSettings — users request approval instead of being blocked outright.

requiredSettings.label: User consent for applications
requiredSettings.value: Allow user consent for apps from verified publishers, for selected permissions (built-in policy microsoft-user-default-low)

requiredSettings.label: Own-resource consent
requiredSettings.value: Users can still consent for resources they own (their own chats / teams they own)

requiredSettings.label: Prerequisite
requiredSettings.value: Classify which permissions count as 'low impact' (Consent and permissions > Permission classifications)

steps: Entra ID > Enterprise apps > Consent and permissions > User consent settings.
Select 'Allow user consent for apps from verified publishers, for selected permissions'.
Under Permission classifications, confirm the low-impact permission set users may consent to.
Save. Anything else routes through the admin consent workflow (see AdminAppConsent).

requiredSettings.label: Self-service sign-up (email-verified / viral)
requiredSettings.value: Disabled — external people can't create accounts in your domain via self-service sign-up

requiredSettings.label: Primary control
requiredSettings.value: Graph authorizationPolicy: allowedToSignUpEmailBasedSubscriptions = false (legacy MSOnline: Set-MsolCompanySettings -AllowEmailVerifiedUsers $false -AllowAdHocSubscriptions $false)

steps: M365 Admin Center > Settings > Org Settings > Services > User-owned apps and services (turn off the self-service sign-up options shown there).

The reliable control is PowerShell/Graph: set allowedToSignUpEmailBasedSubscriptions = false on the authorization policy (or the legacy MSOnline AllowEmailVerifiedUsers / AllowAdHocSubscriptions = $false).

Note: LOW impact / verify-oriented — this blocks self-service/viral account creation on the domain. (Self-service trials & purchases of products is a separate control — see the M365 Admin Settings guidance.)

requiredSettings.label: Enable Microsoft Entra Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS)
requiredSettings.value: Yes (tenant-level enablement)

requiredSettings.label: Also required
requiredSettings.value: A client-side LAPS policy (deployed via Intune) with BackUpDirectory = Microsoft Entra ID — the tenant toggle alone doesn't rotate anything

requiredSettings.label: Scope
requiredSettings.value: Entra joined / hybrid-joined devices only (not Entra-registered); needs the April 2023+ Windows update

steps: Entra ID > Devices > Overview > Device settings.
Set 'Enable Microsoft Entra Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS)' = Yes, Save (needs at least Cloud Device Administrator).
Then deploy the client-side LAPS policy in Intune (Endpoint security > Account protection) with the backup directory set to Microsoft Entra ID.
Retrieve passwords later from Devices > Local administrator password recovery.

requiredSettings.label: Maximum number of devices per user

steps: Entra ID > Devices > Device settings.
Set 'Maximum number of devices per user' = 20.
Save. Note: LOW impact — limits how many devices one user can register/join (join and registered devices count against it).

requiredSettings.label: Users may register their devices with Microsoft Entra
requiredSettings.value: All (allows device registration — required for MDM/MAM enrollment scenarios)

steps: Entra ID > Devices > Device settings.
Set 'Users may register their devices with Microsoft Entra' = All.
Save. Note: LOW impact — this is an enabler (needed for Intune enrollment / BYOD registration), not a restriction.

requiredSettings.label: External user leave settings
requiredSettings.value: Allow external users to leave your organization themselves (Yes)

steps: Entra ID > External Identities > External collaboration settings.
Ensure 'Allow external users to remove themselves from your organization' = Yes.
Save. Note: LOW impact — a privacy/compliance nicety for guests; no effect on internal users.

requiredSettings.label: Security defaults
requiredSettings.value: Disabled — replaced by the tenant's Conditional Access policies

requiredSettings.label: HARD prerequisite
requiredSettings.value: CA MFA policies (MFA for internal users, admins, block legacy auth) must already be deployed and On, or MFA coverage drops to nothing

steps: First confirm the Conditional Access baseline is On (see the CA guidance) — this switch has no safety net of its own.
Entra ID > Overview > Properties > Manage security defaults.
Set Security defaults = Disabled, Save.
Note (impact is LOW if CA is already live, HIGH if not): do this LAST, after the CA policies are validated out of Report-only. Impact rating in the master is deliberately conditional for this reason.
